'''旧暦3月'''は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から3番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[天保暦]]よりも前の定義では、[[穀雨]]を含む月を3月とする。[[新暦]]では[[3月]]下旬から[[5月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
3月の別名は'''弥生'''（'''やよい'''）である。名前の由来は[[3月]]を参照のこと。
3月30日は存在しない年もある）
== 関連項目 == {{月 (暦)|旧暦3月|旧暦360日|月 (旧暦)|3|日 (旧暦)|[[3月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}}

3月の別名は'''弥生'''（'''やよい'''）である。名前の由来は[[3月]]を参照のこと。
3月30日は存在しない年もある）

revision.text:
'''旧暦3月'''（きゅうれきさんがつ）は、[[旧暦]]（[[太陰太陽暦]]）の年初から3番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[天保暦]]よりも前の定義では、[[穀雨]]を含む月を3月とする。[[新暦]]では[[3月]]下旬から[[5月]]上旬ごろに当たる。
3月の別名は'''弥生'''（'''やよい'''）である。名前の由来は[[3月]]を参照のこと。
東洋の太陰太陽暦では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、3月29日までで3月30日は存在しない年もある。

東洋の太陰太陽暦では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、3月29日までで3月30日は存在しない年もある。

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar March}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Grain Rain|ja|穀雨}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|ja|3月}} to early {{ill|May|ja|5月}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|March|ja|3月}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
== Yayoi Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Yayoi Dates ! Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! {{Lunisolar March}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Grain Rain|ja|穀雨|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft}} to early {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.

{{Lunisolar March}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Grain Rain|ja|穀雨|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.

{{Lunisolar March}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Grain Rain|ja|穀雨|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.

{{Lunisolar March}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Grain Rain|ja|穀雨|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.

{{Lunisolar March}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month (Calendar)|ja|月 (暦)|lt=Month|lt_ja=月|12=draft|12=draft}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Grain Rain|ja|穀雨|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|May|ja|5月|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい}}. The origin of the name is explained in {{ill|March|ja|3月|12=draft|12=draft}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
== Yayoi Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Yayoi Dates ! Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! revision.text:
{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|qq=}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|qq=}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|qq=}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|qq=}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|qq=}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|qq=}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Hokkaido Ainu language|ja|北海道アイヌ語|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language|2=ja|3=アイヌ語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Ainu language|qq=}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
== Yayoi Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Yayoi Dates ! Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || [[:en:March 31|March 31]] || [[:en:April 28|April 28]] || 29 days |- | 2015 || [[:en:April 19|April 19]] || [[:en:May 17|May 17]] || 29 days |- | 2016 || [[:en:April 7|April 7]] || [[:en:May 6|May 6]] || 30 days |- | 2017 || [[:en:March 28|March 28]] || [[:en:April 25|April 25]] || 29 days |- | 2018 || [[:en:April 16|April 16]] || [[:en:May 14|May 14]] || 29 days |- | 2019 || [[:en:April 5|April 5]] || [[:en:May 4|May 4]] || 30 days |- | 2020 || [[:en:March 24|March 24]] || [[:en:April 22|April 22]] || 30 days |- | 2021 || [[:en:April 12|April 12]] || [[:en:May 11|May 11]] || 30 days |- | 2022 || [[:en:April 1|April 1]] || [[:en:April 30|April 30]] || 30 days |- | 2023 || [[:en:April 20|April 20]] || [[:en:May 19|May 19]] || 30 days |- | 2024 || [[:en:April 9|April 9]] || [[:en:May 7|May 7]] || 29 days |- | 2025 || [[:en:March 29|March 29]] || [[:en:April 27|April 27]] || 30 days |}

Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.

In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Hokkaido Ainu language|ja|北海道アイヌ語|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language|2=ja|3=アイヌ語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Ainu language|qq=}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|qq=}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|qq=}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|qq=}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|qq=}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|qq=}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|qq=}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Hokkaido Ainu language|ja|北海道アイヌ語|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language|2=ja|3=アイヌ語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Ainu language|qq=}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=New Calendar (Japan)|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|qq=}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|qq=}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|qq=}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|qq=}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|qq=}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|qq=}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|qq=|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|qq=|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|qq=|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|qq=|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|qq=|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|qq=|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|qq=|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|qq=|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|qq=|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

<!--旧暦三月--> <references group="lower-alpha"/> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]] [[za:Ndwensam]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:三月]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]] [[za:Ndwensam]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:三月]] {{wikidata link|Q838927}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
== Yayoi Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Yayoi Dates ! Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || [[:en:March 31|March 31]] || [[:en:April 28|April 28]] || 29 days |- | 2015 || [[:en:April 19|April 19]] || [[:en:May 17|May 17]] || 29 days |- | 2016 || [[:en:April 7|April 7]] || [[:en:May 6|May 6]] || 30 days |- | 2017 || [[:en:March 28|March 28]] || [[:en:April 25|April 25]] || 29 days |- | 2018 || [[:en:April 16|April 16]] || [[:en:May 14|May 14]] || 29 days |- | 2019 || [[:en:April 5|April 5]] || [[:en:May 4|May 4]] || 30 days |- | 2020 || [[:en:March 24|March 24]] || [[:en:April 22|April 22]] || 30 days |- | 2021 || [[:en:April 12|April 12]] || [[:en:May 11|May 11]] || 30 days |- | 2022 || [[:en:April 1|April 1]] || [[:en:April 30|April 30]] || 30 days |- | 2023 || [[:en:April 20|April 20]] || [[:en:May 19|May 19]] || 30 days |- | 2024 || [[:en:April 9|April 9]] || [[:en:May 7|May 7]] || 29 days |- | 2025 || [[:en:March 29|March 29]] || [[:en:April 27|April 27]] || 30 days |}

Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh_min_nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh_yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third [[:en:Month|Month]] of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ([[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]]).
Under definitions preceding the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], March was defined as the month that includes [[:en:Guyu|Grain Rain]]. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:March|March]] to early [[:en:May|May]].
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|[[:en:Cherry blossom|cherry blossom]] ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s [[:en:Prunus × yedoensis|Somei Yoshino]])]] In [[:en:Japan|Japan]], besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the [[:en:Year-ending|fiscal year]] change (primarily the [[:en:Fiscal year|fiscal year]] and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to [[:en:Spring break|spring break]], {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the [[:en:Ainu language|Hokkaido Ainu language]], March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; [[:en:Ainu language|Ainu language]]: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
