revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 3 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 3-ngie̍t]][[ja:3月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월]][[th:เดือน 3]][[wuu:农历三月]][[za:Ndwensam]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 3 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:三月]][[zh:农历三月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q838927]] Wikidata name: 3rd month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name== {{afc comment|1=content here taken from [[:ja:3月]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:24, 11 May 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Kawashiro Gorge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=farewell party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=personnel transfer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain|1=Guyu}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May|1=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom|1=Cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Q17208974|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino|1=Prunus × yedoensis}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year|1=Year-ending}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year|1=Fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=Q56351688|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break|1=Spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=Q11375907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=Town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain|1=Guyu}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May|1=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom|1=Cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Q17208974|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino|1=Prunus × yedoensis}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year|1=Year-ending}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year|1=Fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=Q56351688|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break|1=Spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=Q11375907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=Town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break|1=Spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=Q11375907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=Town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|WD=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|WD=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain|1=Guyu}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|WD=Q119|lt=May|1=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|WD=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom|1=Cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Q17208974|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|WD=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino|1=Prunus × yedoensis}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|WD=Q336568|lt=fiscal year|1=Year-ending}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|WD=Q191891|lt=fiscal year|1=Fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Graduation ceremony|WD=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=Q56351688|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|WD=Q1153162|lt=spring break|1=Spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=Q11375907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=Town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|WD=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|qid=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|qid=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|qid=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain|1=Guyu}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|qid=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|qid=Q119|lt=May|1=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|qid=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom|1=Cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Q17208974|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|qid=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino|1=Prunus × yedoensis}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|qid=Q336568|lt=fiscal year|1=Year-ending}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|qid=Q191891|lt=fiscal year|1=Fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Graduation ceremony|qid=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=Q56351688|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|qid=Q1153162|lt=spring break|1=Spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=Q11375907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=Town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.

{{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''Lunisolar March''' (旧暦三月) is the third {{ill|Month|en|Month|qid=Q5151|lt=Month|1=Month}} of the year in the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}).
Under definitions preceding the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|qid=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, March was defined as the month that includes {{ill|Guyu|en|Guyu|qid=Q1325032|lt=Grain Rain|1=Guyu}}. In the {{ill|New Calendar|ja|新暦|1=The new calendar|lt=New Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/New Calendar (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125314718}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|March|en|March|qid=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} to early {{ill|May|en|May|qid=Q119|lt=May|1=May}}.
Another name for March is {{nihongo|'''Yayoi'''|やよい|qq=}}. An alternative name is “{{nihongo|'''Kishun'''|きしゅん|qq=}}”.
In the Eastern lunisolar calendar, the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies from year to year; consequently, some years have March ending on the 29th, with no March 30th.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
[[File:2014-04-11 Kawashiro-keikoku 川代公園・川代渓谷DSCF6266.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{ill|Cherry blossom|en|Cherry blossom|qid=Q871991|lt=cherry blossom|1=Cherry blossom}} ({{ill|Kawashiro Gorge|ja|川代渓谷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashiro Gorge|1=Q17208974|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17208974}}’s {{ill|Prunus × yedoensis|en|Prunus × yedoensis|qid=Q163078|lt=Somei Yoshino|1=Prunus × yedoensis}})]] In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, besides '''Yayoi''' (やよい) there are alternate names such as '''Flower month''' (花月), '''Auspicious month''' (嘉月), '''Flower-viewing month''' (花見月; はなみづき), '''Dream-viewing month''' (夢見月; ゆめみつき), '''Cherry blossom month''' (桜月; さくらづき, Lunisolar)<ref>{{Kotobank|桜月|デジタル大辞泉}}</ref>, and '''Late spring''' (暮春; ぼしゅん).
In Japan, this month is famous as the period of the {{ill|Year-ending|en|Year-ending|qid=Q336568|lt=fiscal year|1=Year-ending}} change (primarily the {{ill|Fiscal year|en|Fiscal year|qid=Q191891|lt=fiscal year|1=Fiscal year}} and the {{ill|school year|ja|学年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/school year|1=educational year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10291405}}). Throughout the month, {{ill|graduation ceremony|ja|卒業式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/graduation ceremony|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Graduation ceremony|qid=Q11407181}} and {{ill|farewell party|ja|送別会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/farewell party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歓送迎会|1=Q56351688|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56351688}} are held, marking a time of meetings and partings. Also, at the end of the month corresponding to {{ill|Spring break|en|Spring break|qid=Q1153162|lt=spring break|1=Spring break}}, {{ill|personnel transfer|ja|人事異動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/personnel transfer|1=Q11375907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375907}} take place, and many schools, companies, or {{ill|government office|ja|官公庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/government office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 役所|1=Town hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q25550691}} become busy with moving, transitions, and the start of new life.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, March is called “Toetanne” (トエタンネ; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toetanne), meaning “long days,” <ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
